commit.message:
Phase 3B: Channel system — event routing, transports, push notifications

Event router (event_router.rs):
- Route events by EventFilter to multiple targets
- Target types: McpChannel, FileNotification, Webhook, AgentStdin
- Auto-cleanup on agent exit (remove_session)

Transports (transports.rs):
- McpChannelTransport: JSON format for Claude Code MCP channel capability
- FileTransport: JSONL append with timestamps
- WebhookTransport: async HTTP POST with timeout
- StdinTransport: human-readable notification via daemon client

Formatters (formatters.rs):
- Human-readable notifications for StatusChange/AgentExited/TaskUpdate/ContextWarning/LoopDetected
- JSON serialization for programmatic consumption

Channel binary (main.rs):
- CLI args with clap (--socket, --config)
- Connects to daemon, subscribes to all events
- Routes and pushes via configured transports
- TOML config support for default routes and webhooks

788 LOC channel, 67 tests total, clippy clean

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.description: 🤖 CLI tooling for LLM-first GitHub reading and review workflows.

commit.message:
Make the adoption path obvious for new readers

The public README already documented the reusable bootstrap and skills, but a first-time reader still had to scan the page to figure out the fastest path to using it in their own repository.

Add a short copy-paste adoption section near the top with the bootstrap command and the minimal follow-up needed for extra co-authors.

Constraint: Keep the quick-start addition short and avoid re-explaining details already covered later in the README
Rejected: Replace the fuller reuse section | the quick block is complementary, not a substitute for the detailed notes
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep the top-of-file adoption guidance copy-paste friendly; move edge cases to the detailed reuse section below
Tested: reviewed the updated README excerpt locally for clarity and command accuracy
Not-tested: External social preview rendering

Co-authored-by: codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: sample-codex-coauthor
repository.description: Sample Git hook + Codex skills for GitHub co-author attribution

commit.message:
Surface CI status directly in the public README

The repository already had a working verification workflow, but readers landing on the public page had no immediate signal that the bundle was currently passing its self-test.

Add a GitHub Actions status badge near the top of the README and link it to the workflow page.

Constraint: Keep the README change small and avoid duplicating workflow details already documented below
Rejected: Add a separate CI section at the top | the badge is a lighter, more standard signal for repository health
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep the badge URL aligned with the workflow filename and main branch if the workflow is renamed later
Tested: fetched the badge URL and confirmed an HTTP 200 response; checked the updated README header locally
Not-tested: Visual rendering differences outside GitHub

Co-authored-by: codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: sample-codex-coauthor
repository.description: Sample Git hook + Codex skills for GitHub co-author attribution

commit.message:
Keep the public sample repo self-verifying

The repository had become public and reusable, but it still relied on manual local checks to prove that the attribution bundle actually worked after future edits.

Add a reusable verification script plus a GitHub Actions workflow that bootstraps the bundle into a fresh temporary Git repository and confirms a generated commit receives the Codex co-author trailer.

Constraint: The CI check must validate real Git behavior, not just file presence
Rejected: Lint-only workflow | it would miss regressions in bootstrap or commit-message injection behavior
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep CI aligned with the local verification script so repository and workflow checks do the same end-to-end validation
Tested: bash -n on the new verification script; ran the full local verification flow successfully; confirmed actions/checkout latest release is v6.0.2 before pinning the workflow to @v6
Not-tested: Execution of the new workflow inside GitHub Actions after push

Co-authored-by: codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: sample-codex-coauthor
repository.description: Sample Git hook + Codex skills for GitHub co-author attribution

commit.message:
Make the sample attribution setup reusable across repositories

The sample repository had become a good reference, but using it in another project still required manually copying files and remembering which pieces belonged together.

Add a bootstrap script that copies the managed hook, setup script, and repo-local Codex skills into another existing Git repository, then runs the target setup script automatically.

Constraint: Avoid maintaining duplicate template trees with the same hook and skill content
Rejected: Separate template directory with copied files | duplication would drift from the source of truth
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep the bootstrap file list explicit so changes to managed attribution assets remain reviewable
Tested: bash -n for all shell scripts; bootstrap into a fresh temporary Git repo; verified copied files, local Git config, skill paths, and a test commit with the Codex co-author trailer
Not-tested: Overwrite flow with --force against an already customized target repo

Co-authored-by: codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: sample-codex-coauthor
repository.description: Sample Git hook + Codex skills for GitHub co-author attribution

commit.message:
Make attribution health checks one command away

The sample repository could install and manage co-author trailers, but it still lacked a quick read-only health check for confirming that the hook, config, and recent commits were aligned.

Add a third repo-local skill that prints the local hook path, commit template, configured ai.coauthor values, and the co-author trailers on the latest commit.

Constraint: Keep the status check read-only and repository-local
Rejected: Infer status from files alone | Git config and commit history are the actual source of truth
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep this skill diagnostic-only; do not let it mutate Git config or commit history
Tested: bash -n for all shell scripts; ran the status script and verified reported hook path, template, co-authors, and latest trailer
Not-tested: Skill rendering in Codex app or IDE surfaces

Co-authored-by: codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: sample-codex-coauthor
repository.description: Sample Git hook + Codex skills for GitHub co-author attribution

commit.message:
Make additional co-author trailers easy to manage

The repository could install the Codex hook, but it still required raw git config commands to add or fix other co-author entries.

Add a second repo-local skill that lists, adds, and removes ai.coauthor values so people can manage extra trailers without editing hook files or memorizing git config syntax.

Constraint: Do not guess provider emails that are not verified in this repo
Rejected: Hard-code Gemini and Claude presets | unsupported or unverified emails would create misleading defaults
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep provider-specific presets conservative; only bake in values that are verified and intentionally supported
Tested: bash -n for all shell scripts; add/list/remove cycle for a temporary Gemini-formatted entry; verified final ai.coauthor list returned to Codex only
Not-tested: Codex skill selection UX in every surface

Co-authored-by: codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: sample-codex-coauthor
repository.description: Sample Git hook + Codex skills for GitHub co-author attribution

commit.message:
Make Codex attribution setup self-serve inside the sample repo

The repository already demonstrated a deterministic Git hook, but it did not yet provide a Codex-native way to install or repair that setup.

Add a repo-local skill in .agents/skills that wraps the existing setup script so Codex can turn the hook on without relying on memory or ad hoc shell commands.

Constraint: Keep attribution deterministic and repository-local
Rejected: Codex-only skill without a Git hook | commit-time enforcement would remain non-deterministic
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep the Git hook as the enforcement layer; the skill should only install or repair it
Tested: bash -n for the hook, setup script, and skill installer; ran the skill installer and verified local Git config
Not-tested: Codex app or IDE skill-chip rendering

Co-authored-by: codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Phase 3A: Intelligence layer — context rotation, cross-review, auto-recovery

Context monitor (context_monitor.rs):
- Estimates context usage from agent output patterns + byte proxy
- Rotation threshold (default 80%), rotation prompt with task/progress/files/decisions
- Agent-specific max tokens (Claude 200K, Codex 192K, Gemini 1M)

Cross-review engine (review_engine.rs):
- Adversarial cross-model review pairs (Claude↔Codex, Gemini→Claude)
- Structured review prompt with 5 categories (correctness/perf/security/style/arch)
- Parser for VERDICT/ERROR/WARNING/NIT/SUGGESTED FIX format
- needs_changes: Error severity or RequestChanges blocks merge

Auto-recovery (recovery.rs):
- Decision tree: loop→restart, stuck→nudge→restart→escalate, error→retry→escalate
- Tailored nudge/restart prompts with context preservation
- Configurable thresholds (idle 5min, loop 5x, max 3 retries)

Integration:
- PtyManager: health_check(), trigger_rotation(), request_review()
- socket_server: RequestReview handler returns structured ReviewRequest

875 LOC intelligence, 58 tests total, clippy clean

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Update docs for telemetry and trip behavior

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Phase 2B: MCP server (12 tools, JSON-RPC stdio) + task planner

MCP server (rist-mcp):
- main.rs: JSON-RPC 2.0 stdio transport, initialize/tools_list/tools_call
- tools.rs: 12 tool definitions with JSON Schema + daemon-backed handlers
  (spawn_agent, list_agents, get_agent_output, write_to_agent, kill_agent,
   archive_agent, wait_for_idle, run_command, read_task_graph, write_task_graph,
   get_file_ownership, merge_agent)
- MCP protocol: 2024-11-05, structuredContent support

Task planner (ristd):
- planner.rs: TaskPlanner with dependency-aware scheduling
  (ready_tasks, blocked_tasks, assign_task, stats, atomic save/load)
- Integrated into daemon socket_server for ReadTaskGraph/WriteTaskGraph

Daemon client extensions:
- spawn_agent_with_options, archive_agent, wait_for_idle, run_command
- read_task_graph, write_task_graph, preview_merge, merge_agent, get_file_ownership

35 tests passing, clippy clean, 858 LOC MCP + planner

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Phase 2A: Git worktree isolation, file ownership, context injection + 1B fixes

New modules:
- git_manager.rs: Worktree create/remove, merge preview, squash merge, conflict detection (git2 + CLI)
- file_ownership.rs: Declare/release/check ownership, save/load JSON, conflict errors
- context_injector.rs: RISTRETTO.md generation with agent identity, peers, task deps, rules

Phase 1B fixes (from #8):
- daemon_client.rs: Type-safe DaemonFrame tagged enum replaces serde_json::Value discrimination
- socket_server.rs: Subscribe request now returns Response::Ok
- socket_server.rs: GetFileOwnership, MergeAgent, ArchiveAgent fully implemented

Integration:
- PtyManager: spawn_agent creates worktrees, declares ownership, writes RISTRETTO.md
- PtyManager: archive_agent releases ownership, optionally cleans worktree
- 27 tests passing, clippy clean, 2369 LOC in ristd

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
BIG-PAR-219: github-sync status relation metrics (#152)

* BIG-PAR-103: fix repo-root path resolution for workspace validate

* BIG-PAR-103: record tracker closeout

* BIG-PAR-103: append final tracker note

* BIG-PAR-103: fail closed on lease expiry and surface lease-loss

* BIG-PAR-103: tracker progress update

* fix(queue): harden shared-queue lease safety (BIG-PAR-103)

Summary:
- Serialize SQLite leasing with BEGIN IMMEDIATE to prevent double-lease races.
- Enforce lease ownership + attempt checks for file/memory queue operations.
- Reject requeue/dead-letter transitions once a task is cancelled/dead-lettered.
- Add a regression test covering concurrent LeaseNext across two SQLite clients.

Rationale:
- Multi-worker and multi-node runs share one SQLite queue file. Deferred
  transactions could allow multiple processes to select and lease the same
  task before either UPDATE executes.

Tests:
- cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* BIG-PAR-103: purge cancelled leases after expiry

* BIG-PAR-103: tracker progress update (expiry purge)

* BIG-PAR-103: expose lease-loss metrics

Phase 1B: TUI client with ratatui — sidebar, split panes, daemon client

- app.rs: Full app state (agents, focus, layout modes, input buffer, event handling)
- ui.rs: Sidebar with status indicators (●◐◉⊘○✓✗), split pane layout, task panel, help overlay, status bar
- daemon_client.rs: Async Unix socket client with reconnect, event subscription, request/response multiplexing
- event.rs: Crossterm event handling, all keybindings (N/K/1-9/Tab/D/P/?/Enter/Esc/Ctrl-C/Q)
- main.rs: CLI args, terminal init/restore, panic hook, tick-based refresh (1s agents, 500ms output)
- CJK text width via unicode-width, i18n integration
- 1227 LOC TUI, clippy clean, all tests pass

commit.message:
Phase 1A review fixes: adapter trait, VecDeque ring buffer, event broadcast

- Add AgentAdapter trait + ClaudeCode/Codex/Gemini/Default implementations
- PtyManager now uses adapters to build real agent commands
- RingBuffer switched from Vec<u8> to VecDeque<u8> for O(1) drain
- drain_all() renamed to snapshot() (no mutation)
- check_exits() polls child processes, updates status/exit_code
- Event broadcast: StatusChange + AgentExited emitted on state transitions
- refresh_runtime_state() detects idle/working/stuck/waiting from output
- Loop detection (repeated line ×3)
- Deprecated flock warning suppressed
- 12 tests passing, clippy clean

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(workflow): normalize branch and PR checks

Summary:
- strip worktree suffixes like `@<sha>` when deriving Symphony branch
  names from workspace directories
- replace scratch-file `.git` write probes with `git rev-parse`
  plus `test -w` guidance
- make PR lookup use the live current branch with one issue-key
  fallback before concluding no PR exists

Rationale:
- keep workflow automation aligned with the checked-out repository
  even when Linear metadata or workspace folder names drift
- avoid false blockers caused by brittle Git metadata probes and
  branch-name mismatches during PR discovery

Tests:
- not run (workflow/docs-only change)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(feed): variant-aware GMC feed with item_group_id grouping (#805)

* feat(feed): add variant-aware GMC feed with item_group_id grouping

Emit one <item> per product variant for merchants with gmc_variants_enabled,
grouped by item_group_id. Maps color/storage to GMC variant attributes,
adds product_detail enrichment from key_specs, and preserves ?variant=
deep links through all storefront redirects.

- Add gmc_variants_enabled flag on merchants table (migration)
- Create variant-mapping utility (JSONB attrs → GMC color/size/product_detail)
- Extend feed builder with variant emission, dedup, and canonical_link
- Fetch variants via RPC + key_specs gated by merchant flag
- Propagate variant/spec query errors instead of silently swallowing
- XML-escape product_detail values to prevent malformed feeds
- Restore category-text fallback for legacy product URLs
- Preserve ?variant= in all 3 redirect paths (category, lowercase, products/)
- Add ?variant= preselection in both storefront templates (ogabassey + generic)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(web): update tests for RPC-based variant fetching and description source

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(feed): address follow-up CodeRabbit review findings

- Consistent 'Baci Store' fallback branding in metadata
- Rename _resolvedSearchParams → resolvedSearchParams
- Handle array variant param safely in redirects
- Add image pre-selection for variant color deep links
- Remove redundant .toString() in escapeXmlValue
- Remove dead _getProductVariantsSelect helper
- Add window.location cleanup in product-detail-client tests
- Add explicit VariantRow/SpecsRow types in feed route

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review findings in PR #723

* fix(feed): respect manage_stock in GMC availability

* fix: remove incomplete product page changes from GMC feed PR

The original PR introduced product page imports for modules
(renderable-product-description, product-page-related-content) that
were never created. Reset product pages to main versions and keep
only the core GMC feed variant-mapping feature.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: tighten cached-data assertions for PR #805

* fix: address GMC feed review findings in PR #805

commit.message:
fix flappy bird template: physics, collisions, scoring, and config merge

Rewrites the flappy bird template to fix multiple bugs:
- Deep merge config instead of shallow spread (nested defaults were lost)
- Use body.reset() for pipe collider position sync (bodies were stuck at 0,0)
- Circular bird hitbox for fairer collision detection
- Pipe recycling pool instead of unsafe forEach-destroy iteration
- Use collider (not overlap) for pipes/ground to prevent pass-through
- Phaser zones for score detection instead of hacky undefined sprite cast
- State machine enum replacing fragile boolean flags
- Proper shutdown() lifecycle instead of removeAllListeners()
- Delta-time bird rotation for framerate independence
- Dynamic gravity from config instead of hardcoded in Phaser setup

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(security): add missing CSRF protection to mutating API endpoints (#809)

* fix(security): add missing CSRF protection to mutating API endpoints

Replaces closed PR #701.

Adds `checkCsrfProtection()` from `@/lib/csrf` to 55 mutating API
routes (POST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH) that were missing CSRF validation.

The existing `checkCsrfProtection` utility already handles:
- Skipping Bearer token auth (mobile apps)
- Skipping webhook and auth endpoints
- Double Submit Cookie pattern with constant-time comparison

Excluded from this change:
- Agentic routes (API-key auth, not cookie-based)
- Analytics tracker routes (server-side event forwarding)
- Storefront customer-facing routes (separate auth pattern)
- Parameterless cron/worker endpoints (no request to validate)
- Routes that already had CSRF protection

Also adds CSRF mocks to 11 existing test files that now need them.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(test): add missing CSRF mock to ship-on-credit test

The CSRF protection added to the route requires mocking checkCsrfProtection
in the test, otherwise all POST requests return 403 before reaching handler logic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add `.error` checking to the Supabase `insert()` query for `order_payment_accounts` in the `ship-on-credit` route. commit.message:
fix(well-known): use Host header for domain detection on Vercel (#799)

* fix(well-known): use Host header instead of nextUrl.hostname

On Vercel, request.nextUrl.hostname returns the internal deployment
hostname, not the custom domain. Use request.headers.get('host')
to correctly detect ogabassey.com vs usebaci.com.

Also reduce s-maxage to 60s since Vercel CDN doesn't vary cache
by Host header — a long s-maxage serves the wrong domain's response.

commit.message:
fix(well-known): add Vary Host header and sanitize ROOT_DOMAIN (#793)

* fix(well-known): add Vary Host header and sanitize ROOT_DOMAIN newlines

Vercel edge cache was sharing the same assetlinks.json response across
all custom domains because the route lacked a Vary: Host header.
Additionally, a corrupted NEXT_PUBLIC_ROOT_DOMAIN env var (with embedded
newlines) caused hostname matching to fail. This adds newline
sanitization as a defensive measure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(well-known): handle CRLF in ROOT_DOMAIN and add corruption tests

Use /[\r\n]/ split instead of '\n' to handle Windows-style line endings.
Add tests verifying getAppConfigForDomain correctly sanitizes both LF
and CRLF-corrupted rootDomain values.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: Baci
repository.description: Experiment

commit.message:
fix(build): remove import.meta.url, revert --webpack, simplify csrf (#795)

* fix(build): remove import.meta.url from next.config.ts

PR 791 added import.meta.url to compute __dirname, but this ESM-only
syntax causes Node 24's auto-detection to treat the SWC-compiled CJS
output as ESM, crashing with "exports is not defined". Since the
compiled output is CJS, __dirname is available natively.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(build): add webpack fallback for node:crypto in client bundles

csrf.ts uses a dynamic import('node:crypto') behind a runtime Node
check. When building with --webpack (PR 790), webpack statically
resolves this even in client bundles, causing UnhandledSchemeError.
Map node:crypto to false for client builds so it resolves to an empty
module — the runtime isNodeRuntime check prevents it from being called.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(build): use resolve.alias for node:crypto client fallback

resolve.fallback doesn't intercept node: URI scheme imports — webpack
fails at scheme parsing before reaching fallback resolution. Use
resolve.alias instead, which intercepts before scheme handling.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(build): remove node:crypto from csrf.ts, use Web Crypto API only

The dynamic import('node:crypto') in csrf.ts caused webpack
UnhandledSchemeError when building client bundles (--webpack flag).
Web Crypto API (crypto.subtle) is available in Node 18+ and all modern
browsers, making the Node-specific branch unnecessary. This removes the
only node:crypto usage in client-bundled code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(build): revert --webpack flag, use Turbopack (Next.js 16 default)

PR 790 added --webpack to fix a VPS OOM during Turbopack builds.
However, webpack 5 cannot handle node: URI scheme imports in client
bundles (webpack#14166 — still open, resolve.fallback and resolve.alias
do not work with node: prefix). Multiple server-only files
(kuda.ts, csrf.ts, etc.) use import ... from 'node:crypto' and are
transitively imported by 'use client' components.

Turbopack handles node: imports correctly and is the Next.js 16 default
bundler. The deploy workflow already sets NODE_OPTIONS=--max_old_space_size=8192
which should prevent OOM. If memory issues recur, the fix should be
memory tuning, not switching bundlers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): remove setup-node pnpm cache to prevent false-positive failures

The deploy workflow nukes the pnpm store each run (rm -rf) to fix
cross-runner install issues. This means setup-node's Post Run cache
save step always fails with "Path Validation Error" since the
originally-detected store path no longer exists after the nuke+recreate
cycle. Remove cache: "pnpm" since it provides no benefit when the store
is recreated from scratch every run.

Also hoist PNPM_STORE_DIR to job-level env so it's visible to all steps
without needing an inline export.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(build): add Turbopack resolveAlias for tiptap compat shim

The webpack() callback is dead code when building with Turbopack
(Next.js 16 default). Add turbopack.resolveAlias to ensure the
@tiptap/extension-text-style compat shim is applied during builds.

Keep the webpack() callback as fallback for --webpack flag usage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(build): use relative path in Turbopack resolveAlias

Turbopack doesn't support absolute paths from path.resolve() in
resolveAlias — it prepends './' making them invalid relative imports.
Use a relative path from the project root instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(symphony): add rotating log files under --logs-root

Summary:
- add a new logging module that builds a size-based rotating file appender
  writing to `<logs-root>/log/symphony.log` with a 10MB cap and 5 total files
- wire CLI startup tracing to duplicate JSON events to stdout and the rotating
  file writer when `--logs-root` is provided
- retain and explicitly drop the non-blocking worker guard before process exit
  so queued file logs flush reliably on startup failures
- add CLI regression tests for both configured `--logs-root` dual-output
  behavior and default stdout-only behavior without `--logs-root`

Rationale:
- match the Elixir LogFile behavior while preserving existing stdout logging
  semantics for default runs
- make file logging deterministic by creating the log directory before appender
  construction and draining queued writes before process exit

Tests:
- cd apps/symphony && cargo test
- cd apps/symphony && cargo clippy -- -D warnings

field. Rename unused ESLint/Jest configs to .mjs to preserve their ESM

commit.message:
fix(symphony): address dashboard review feedback

Summary:
- Treat null or undefined values in `formatDuration` as `n/a` so polling
  tiles do not render misleading `0s` placeholders.
- Use a single retry-delay value source (`retry_after_ms` then `due_in_ms`)
  for both retry row sorting and displayed delay text.
- Initialize polling duration tiles with `n/a` placeholders and assert this in
  dashboard shell tests to avoid stale mixed formatting before refresh.

Rationale:
- Inline review flagged edge-case inconsistencies between rendered values and
  sort/format behavior, especially when nullable fields are present.
- Aligning fallback rules and initial placeholders makes the dashboard output
  deterministic and consistent with the refresh formatter.

Tests:
- cd apps/symphony && cargo fmt --check && cargo test && cargo clippy -- -D warnings

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: kata
repository.description: Kata multi-agent orchestration monorepo: CLI, Symphony, Desktop, Context, Orchestrator

commit.message:
feat(symphony): enrich HTTP dashboard state views

Summary:
- Replace the dashboard's raw live-state JSON pane with structured sections
  for running sessions, retry queue entries, completed issues, token totals,
  and polling diagnostics.
- Keep 2-second polling and add client-side render helpers for elapsed time,
  retry timing, empty states, and collapsible rate-limit JSON.
- Update dashboard HTTP tests and Symphony AGENTS endpoint docs to reflect the
  richer UI shell.

Rationale:
- The previous dashboard made issue-level state hard to inspect quickly
  because all details were buried in raw JSON.
- Structured tables/lists surface the operational signal directly while
  preserving existing API contracts and refresh behavior.

Tests:
- cd apps/symphony && cargo fmt --check && cargo test && cargo clippy -- -D warnings

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
meta: record shared-memory console-ring integration validation

This commit records completion/validation of the recent shared-memory and
console-ring integration work:

- native driver API now exposes shmem_create/shmem_map/shmem_unmap plus
  console_ring_id
- serial->framebuffer text forwarding moved from IPC text messages to a
  shared-memory console ring consumed by framebuffer_native
- WASM syscall surface extended with shmem_create/shmem_map/shmem_unmap
- shared-memory internals gained physical lookup and explicit retain/release
  ownership hooks
- framebuffer text IPC PUT_CHAR/PUT_STRING path removed in favor of ring drain
- README.md and ARCHITECTURE.md updated to reflect current behavior

Validation:
- cmake --build build --target run-qemu-test (pass)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(docs): add GitHub Pages deployment workflow

Summary:
- add a GitHub Actions workflow to build and deploy VitePress docs
  to GitHub Pages
- restrict automatic runs to pushes on main that touch docs files or
  the workflow itself
- set the VitePress base path from GitHub Actions metadata so asset
  URLs work under repository Pages paths

Rationale:
- publish docs automatically without running the workflow for every
  push or pull request
- keep the local docs experience unchanged while making Pages
  deployments work for project sites

Tests:
- pnpm -C docs docs:build

commit.message:
docs(extensions): define plugin and MCP strategy

Summary:
- add a detailed plugin and MCP strategy doc covering Seraph's current extension surfaces, reference system patterns, MCP risks, and the recommended typed-extension architecture
- link the new strategy from the paired research and implementation ecosystem docs so the plan is discoverable from the existing roadmap structure

Rationale:
- Seraph already has multiple extension seams, but they were not documented as one coherent execution strategy
- MCP needed a specific product recommendation grounded in current security, packaging, and UX concerns rather than hand-wavy pro/con discussion

Tests:
- cd docs && npm run build
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
